plain_message: Employment records must not store unprotected personally identifiable information. Encrypt sensitive fields like SSN, date of birth, and home address before persisting.
Filtering or scoring candidates by protected characteristics (age, race, gender, religion, disability) violates EEOC guidelines. Remove or replace this logic immediately.
Salary and compensation data must not be written to logs. Log an anonymised identifier instead.
Background check results and criminal history data must be encrypted at rest and access-logged. Add field-level encryption before storing.
Sending employee personal data to a third-party service requires a Data Processing Agreement. Verify the recipient is compliant before transmitting.
Employment records tables should have a defined retention schedule. EEOC requires records kept for at least 1 year; ADEA for 3 years. Add a retention or archival mechanism.
